response: The three main psychedelic compounds found in Psilocybin genus mushrooms that are converted to psilocin by the body are psilocybin, psilocin, and baeocystin. Psilocybin is quickly converted by the body to psilocin, which has mind-altering effects similar to those of LSD, mescaline, and DMT. Baeocystin is an "N"-demethylated derivative of psilocybin and a phosphorylated derivative of 4-HO-NMT, and it is also converted to psilocin. Psilocin is the active compound that produces the psychoactive effects associated with Psilocybin mushrooms.
